reviews.text: I had a lawyer through here for a couple years. While he had many clients he definitely came in ready to argue in my favor whenever my file was in rotation.  I see a not so hot review on here;Wouldn't take it to heart. People need to understand these lawyers are not at your beck and call. They have many cases and are humans,not service robots.  Mr.Kazakis always made sure to keep me updated and showed he's one of the good ones. The office is lovely, Receptionist is always kind. Good find.

Never informed me of anything. Was hard to get a hold of and never told me my license was suspended and almost got arrested again because he failed to let me know fully what was going on. I don't recommend. Won't return your calls for his mistake. Missed several tickets had to come in to tell him that not worth the 3k

I was charged with a crime i never committed here but there was enough evidence to make me look guilty to some. I was recommended mark furlan by another client of his and he make one phone call and the charges where dropped. Hands down the best lawyer I’ve ever had. Highly recommend!

Kevin was kind enough to walk me through some property concerns, questions etc.  He was patient and thorough, and professional. I would recommend him and would be happy to work with him on projects in the future.
reviews.ownerResponse: Thanks so much, Doug! We appreciate your recommendation and look forward to working with you again!

reviews.text: Kevin and Jason were awesome to work with. Quick to response and very helpful and was able to ease my mind!!!
reviews.ownerResponse: Thankyou Eythan!

reviews.text:
I should've written this much sooner. Kevin is a man of absolute character. He saw me and my friend Dan attempting to shovel out his stranded car on the access road. He came, on foot, with two shovels all the way from his firm down to the base of the road. He worked hard for the 45-60 minutes it took us to finally free the car, and was nothing short of polite and helpful the whole time.
Although I don't (and hopefully wont) need legal help in the future, I hope anyone who does speaks to Kevin and his firm. I will vouch for his character, and selflessness.
Thank you Kevin
reviews.ownerResponse: Thanks so much, Sam! We’re glad we could help and truly appreciate your kind words. Wishing you and Dan all the best!

reviews.text: Kevin is an absolutely amazing lawyer, but this is not why I’m writing this review. I’ve known him for almost 2 years and he’s helped me out greatly with my legal issues. But the real reason I’m writing this review is because Kevin is a great person. This morning I was driving to work and the roads were real slick and I ended up loosing control and drove into a snowbank. This was right down the street of the firm when Kevin happened to be heading into to work as well. He recognized my car and quickly grabbed two shovels. After nearly an hour of shoveling we were able to dig my car out of the snow. Kevin is an amazing person and someone you can depend on. Now I know for a fact that I will not be reaching out to anyone other than Kevin for legal assistance!
reviews.ownerResponse: Thanks Daniel! Glad we could get you out and on your way! We're always here to help!

They were not only professional but were incredibly responsive. Justin had eagle eyes and caught some issues with my survey that easily could have been missed! He was also incredibly flexible with us as we were slow to respond due to conflicts with work/travel schedules on our end. We also appreciated their use of Closing Lock which make our closing process seamless! We were able to close while on a vacation abroad with no issues sending through our POA or funds.

We plan to invest more around the mountain in the near future and will always use this team for our transactions!
reviews.ownerResponse: Thank you for trusting us with your complex land purchase! We’re glad we could make the process smooth even while you were abroad. We look forward to assisting with your future investments!

reviews.text: I was in a car crash while on vacation in South Carolina. A driver ran a red light and broad sided us in an intersection. Both of us were injured: my husband a broken leg and I had a broken rib and stitches. The other driver was driving a rental vehicle also. We were diligent about completing all of the paperwork that was sent and working with the insurance companies. Nine months later we received a bill for the car we were driving. The other rental agency was not returning calls and refusing to pay anything. We hired Dan at this point and later learned that our emergency room bills had been sent to collections. The bills were over $100,000. Dan was able to negotiate with the hospitals, insurance agencies and we received a settlement. Dan gave us an estimated timeline and communicated with us regularly. He was and his staff are easy to work with and quick to reply with any questions we had.

My family retained  Cindy Broadfoot for my daughter's divorce and a recent need to change the visitation status of the original decree from four years ago. Cindy helped us navigate contentious behavior from the opposing side,  and helped us not lose sight of what our goals were. She was quick to answer our calls and emails. Both cases were stressful and difficult; yet, we got the results we wanted each time. Taking legal action is expensive and emotionally draining; with Cindy on our side we got the results we wanted, and felt cared for during the process.

I recently worked with Cindy and her team on divorce related matters and they were an excellent team to work with. I felt well informed and fully supported through the whole process. Both Cindy and Deborah took time to answer all of my questions while working quickly and efficiently to move things forward. I felt like these women understood where I was coming from and validated my feelings through some very tough times. I  would highly recommend this law firm to anyone that is seeking legal assistance for custody and divorce/separation.

She also wasn't afraid to tell me the truth, even when I didn't want to hear it, so I could anticipate what was coming. I have already recommended her to a friend and will continue to do so. I felt I had a true advocate with me on my divorce journey.

I don’t know what I would have done without Cindy. It’s a new low to be mentally and physically drained, while also trying to pull evidence and know what my next steps are in a court case, especially never experiencing the judicial system prior. Cindy was this light I just continued following until the case was over, with a result I was content with not to mention. I remember feeling my chest tight, heart racing, but when I looked up in the virtual courtroom, and saw she was there, I felt a wave of calm and knew I would be just fine. I hope to never need her again (respectfully) but if i happen to need an attorney, it will be her.
Cynthia was amazing to work with through a difficult time. Her and Her team were responsive, professional and provided much needed guidance for many topics. Cannot reccomend enough!
Cindy is a professional in every way. Her second opinion gave me a crucial boost of confidence in reaching a settlement.
Cindy Broadfoot is a wonderful lawyer who cares deeply about favorable results and her clients. I highly recommend her!

Cynthia was very informative and assisted me with navigating a hefty settlement. She was very responsive, professional and knowledgeable. I appreciate her efforts as it saved both side a lot of time and money.

So very happy with Cindy’s professionalism during my divorce.  There were times that I got very frustrated and she had a way of calming me down. In the end she did an amazing job for me once I realized she was looking out for my best interests.  Highly recommend!

Cynthia Broadfoot - A True Professional in Divorce Law

I recently had the privilege of working with Cindy during my divorce, and I cannot emphasize enough how grateful I am for her exceptional legal representation and support throughout the entire process. From the very first consultation, Cindy demonstrated an in-depth understanding of divorce law and a compassionate approach to my unique situation. She took the time to listen to my concerns, assess the complexities of my case, and provided honest and realistic expectations about the possible outcomes. Throughout the process, communication with Cindy was outstanding. I always felt well-informed about the progress of my case, and she promptly responded to any questions or concerns I had along the way. This level of responsiveness and attention to detail gave me the confidence that my case was in the hands of a competent and dedicated professional.

Cindy proved to be a highly skilled negotiator during settlement discussions, advocating for my best interests while keeping the focus on finding an amicable resolution whenever possible. Thanks to her expertise and strategic approach, we were able to reach a favorable settlement without the need for a lengthy and emotionally draining court battle. Furthermore, I was impressed by the level of empathy and understanding shown by Cindy. Going through a divorce is an emotionally challenging experience, but she was there every step of the way, providing not just legal guidance, but also emotional support. The legal fees were reasonable and transparent, and I never felt that I was being charged unnecessarily or unfairly. Cindy was upfront about the costs from the beginning and provided detailed invoices that made it easy to track the expenses.

Overall, I cannot recommend Cynthia Broadfoot highly enough for anyone in need of a divorce attorney. If you are looking for a professional who combines legal expertise, outstanding communication, and genuine care for her clients, Cindy is the one you can trust.

Thank you, Cindy, for helping me navigate through this challenging chapter of my life and securing the best possible outcome for me and my family.
Cindy has helped us with numerous legal matters. She listens to your needs and talks you through the process so there are no “big” surprises. Lucky for us we have never had a bad outcome with Cindy!

Cynthia Broadfoot is an excellent lawyer who knows the law well. She and her paralegal staff (specifically Jennifer Keser) did an exceptional job with my divorce case. Cynthia took the time to hear all of my concerns and clearly, concisely outlined all the different courses of action including the positives and negatives with each choice. Furthermore, Cynthia and her staff always responded to me in a timely manner. I could not have asked for better representation, and working with Cynthia
was incredibly easy, and the least stressful part of a very difficult, painful event in my life. Without hesitation, I give Cynthia Broadfoot my highest recommendation.

Cindy Broadfoot is an excellent attorney.  By the time I met Cindy I felt defeated and exhausted from the drama of my situation.  I mentioned my concerns about money and the possibility of my custody relocation case dragging on.  Cindy listened and worked very diligently in order to even keep that a possibility - which I greatly appreciated.  She consistently redirected me away from the drama which kept me focused and on track, making my path clear and concise.  Always treating me with the utmost respect and compassion as to what I may be going through.

Cindy explained VT. law in layman's terms without being dismissive or belittling.  This gave me the confidence I needed throughout the many tough times one goes through during a custody case.  I always felt she had the time and patience for me.  In a sea full of lawyers that's a rarity these days.  I'm so happy I found her and so happy she was on my side.  I highly recommend seeking counsel with Broadfoot Attorneys at Law.

Fabulous lawyers! Cynthia and Deb were helpful and supportive during my 6 years of divorce trials with a narcissistic ex. I am thankful! They treated me like family. All went in my favor with their assistance.
never called back
Best Divorce Lawyer in the State - she even co-wrote a book about divorce in Vermont.

My granddaughter’s mother was (sadly) addicted to some powerful drugs, and had put her daughter in harms way.    Attorney Broadfoot (and her staff) exceeded our expectations.  On September 8 my son was awarded sole and legal custody for our granddaughter.   I came to this law firm on a recommendation from my daughter.  My daughter also had top notch aid from this law firm when she went through her divorce.  We would never hesitate for one moment to retain this law firm should we need help in future endeavors.

reviews.text: Great attorney,  highly skilled

First the bad reviews here are nuts. They are irrelevant and Google should remove them. Personally, I think the hourly for ALL lawyers is insane, but relatively these folks are reasonable and don't waste time. I don't like that I always have to leave messages, but staffing is tough for all businesses and more staff means clients will pay more. I have worked with all of the lawyers in this firm and Nigel. They are responsive and experienced and kind. I have done closings, corporate documents, tenant issues and a will with them. I've never felt disappointed or overcharged. I worked with Chip for so many years and have referred clients to him and the other lawyers in the firm. I saw him as a friend. I am so sorry to hear of his passing.

I was looking for a lawyer so the VT Bar assigned me Ward law. At first, Ward law did not return calls. After several calls, they finally returned my call. Per the VT bar they were to give me 30 minutes of legal advice for $25 for the initial client contact. Nigel spent the entire 30 minutes describing why I need to pay every time a Lawyer breaths. Not 1 piece of legal advice was given. The simple question of do I have a case was not answered. They claimed they needed to research the law, and bill me, to determine if I have a case. I was horrified by the greed from this law firm and am looking for a way to report them to the VT bar for not giving me the 30 minutes of legal time they are required to to be a part of the VT bar association. They will freely take your money and provide you 0 legal advice.
5 stars are not nearly enough to acknowledge Chip's knowledge, guidance and patience.  Thank you, Chip.  Regards, Jeff
reviews.ownerResponse: Thanks for the compliment!  Building long-term relationships with our clients are at the core of the firm's mission to provide solid legal advice and support to our clients.  We want to be able to provide big firm experience and skills but with a small firm’s commitment to your individual needs.  Glad we could be of assistance.  Ward Law, PC

reviews.text: I've been seeking the assistance of attorney Will Towle and the other fine folks at Ward Law, PC for the better part of 12 years. They are ethical, kind and utterly professional, and I have only been very well served by the advice, representation and legal assistance they have provided on numerous occasions. I highly recommend they be your first choice for legal assistance in the state of Vermont.

If I could give no stars I would. I went above and beyond for chip on an application giving him documents for tenancy to be turned away (for no real reason). I asked for an honest answer on what made us not rentable.... we received nothing back. We had been rebuilding our credit had several thousand dollars in the bank (proved with statements) just to be shot down. He discriminated against us and stereotyped us for being young. Sorry we don’t drive around an Audi however we work very hard and pay our rent on time. Unless you want someone who is going to look down upon you DO NOT rent from him.
reviews.ownerResponse:
Jessica, you have never done business with our law firm. Your review is about applying to rent a house. We do not rent houses. We are a law firm.

Chip, one of our lawyers, personally manages a house which you apparently applied to be a tenant. This is unrelated to our law firm. It sounds like you were disappointed that you did not get the house, but it has nothing to do with our law firm or the services we provide.

An owner decides on an application based on information owners can gather about applicants. Poor credit, inadequate and unverifiable self employment income, being a smoker, a history of suing a past landlord, and generally not being forthright about rental history — these are all reasons a prospective tenant is not likely to be accepted by an owner.
Thank you!

As a family we all agree to practice the following CDC recommended safe physical distancing guidelines to help keep everyone safe!

Remain at least 6 feet away from people outside our family approved circle.

Wear masks when inside stores, when at work, or in other indoor public places.

Wear masks when outside in public places when it is not possible to always stay 6 or more feet away from other people.

Wash hands after touching things that many people outside our circle may have touched (a few examples: after going inside stores, handling mail, etc.)

Let other family members know if you have had contact with people outside the circle (without masks and 6 ft distancing) ahead of meeting up with them to allow family time to make their own decisions about getting together.

Pretty ableist statement from a law firm that purportedly helps disabled people help get disability benefits. Highly recommend NOT supporting this business, this is unacceptable.
reviews.ownerResponse: Jessica, I'm sorry that we were not able to help you. Although age is a significant factor in a disability claim, we never turn away a potential client merely because of their age. If we do not take a case, it is usually because we cannot get a supporting medical opinion from a treating physician. The important factors in a successful disability claim are age, the medical evidence, and the judge you draw, with medical evidence being the most important. Medical evidence includes regular treatment for the conditions which are disabling and supporting opinions from the doctors who treat you. The supporting opinions need to be more than the doctor saying, "this patient is disabled." The opinions must give specific limitations and reasons why you cannot work. For someone under 50 years old, the opinions must show that the person meets or equals a "medical listing" or cannot do any sort of full-time work. These can be challenging hurdles to overcome. On the bright side, even if we cannot develop supporting medical evidence initially, we are always willing to take a second look at a case if the medical picture changes. For example, if your doctor would not give us an opinion initially but later changes her mind, we are always happy to take a look at what your doctor says and see if it would help your case.

reviews.text: I reached out to Meub associates to help me with a closing on a house. My previous lawyer had unexpectedly passed away in the middle of my closing. Meub said they’d be happy to help. There was a little more involved in this closing so I expected maybe $1500-$2000 in legal fees. Meub charged me over $4100, I was shocked. After looking over the bill, they would charge me for 30 minute phone calls when my calls would rarely go over 5 minutes. Who knows what else they up charged me for. It was such a stressful time trying to find a new lawyer after mine had passed, I can’t believe Meaub would try to take advantage and capitalize on a fellow colleague’s death.
reviews.ownerResponse: Mr. Schu’s statements are generally false.  We agreed to help out and take over for Mr. Layden after he died, because Mr. Layden was a good friend and former partner.  Mr. Schu had purchased a duplex at a foreclosure sale.  The confirmation order had to be revised and reissued and then served.  The foreclosing bank was difficult to get timely response from, which upset Mr. Schu and caused issues.  The owner and tenants were still in possession of the property, and we had to get Mr. Schu possession of the property for a closing, which is what he agreed to do when he signed the agreement at the foreclosure sale.  Serving and having to get writs of possession enforced added costs.  His costs also included title insurance.  The actual amount of time involved exceeded what he was charged.  There were also other issues involved further complicating things.  This matter was not a simple real estate purchase

I found the Attorney’s advice to be quite helpful in terms of directing me to appropriate resources and endeavoring to save me unnecessary legal fees, and in a manner that was blessedly succinct and concise compared to other lawyers I’ve consulted.

Harold Stevens was extremely short and rude over the phone when requesting initial information. Before he would explain his credentials or anything in the way of niceties, he explained that "His time is money" and will bill $75 for an initial consultation, even though the firm’s website claims the initial consultation is free. When I asked if he handled cases like mine he said "Bye bye" and hung up. Sir, everyone's time is valuable, not just yours, and I would recommend you consider manners and kindness when dealing with the public.
Brooks McArthur was amazing and I am beyond grateful for him. He was reliable, caring, and consistent. I highly recommend!
Brooks McArthur kept fighting for me every step of the way. I can’t thank him enough for the dedication, persistence, and clear communication throughout such a tough case. Highly recommend!

Some people do their jobs because it is a call to serve, to really help others who are in need of their guidance,  in need of their knowledge and expertise.

My daughter and I needed help after being in a horrific car crash on Interstate 89. I couldn't walk without pain. My daughter,  a musician,  started dropping her drumsticks when she played her instrument.

I emailed Brooks McArthur at Gravel & Shea. He called me and invited us to his office. We sat with Brooks and Cassie Parah, the Paralegal. They welcomed us, listened to us, explained the process for dealing with the legal aspects of every step (for over a year).

It was a huge relief to know that they were taking care of me and my daughter, while we navigated doctors appointments and therapy sessions, as our bodies healed.

Brooks, Cassie, and the folks at Gravel & Shea helped us have peace of mind and spirit so our bodies could heal faster. There was less stress knowing that they were handling things behind the scenes legally.

They treated us with kindness, professionalism, and patience. Most importantly, they treated us like human beings who were going through an extremely uncertain, scary, and difficult time in our lives.

We are forever grateful to them. We know who to call for any future legal concerns in the future and we recommend those who need legal services to reach out to them too.

Sincerely

Brian Breedlove, ESQ, is a very knowledgeable and professional man. He accepted my case in New York, while I was living in Florida. We worked most of my case out over email and phone. I had to fly to NY to get my warrant quashed. Before we even met in person, he solved 99% of my case without me there. Simply awesome. I thought my case would go on and on, but Brian made it go away with one appearance. I would definitely recommend him to my family and friends.

Mr.Breedlove is professional, knowledgeable, efficient and effective. He made a difficult and stressful time hopeful. Working with him is one of the best decisions I have made. I highly recommend hiring Mr.Breedlove as your representation.

Mr. Breedlove did an excellent job defending me. He got my case reduced with minimum fines by the court. I would definitely refer him to anyone who needs a great defense attorney with many years of knowledge and experience

One of the worst experiences that I’ve had with an attorney. We sold our home and the buyers used this law office. This attorney told us that they were “not going do our lawyer’s job for them” and help sign the paperwork so we had to drive an additional hour and a half to get everything done. We had to sit around for 30 minutes, waiting for the buyers to sign paperwork (when our paperwork is already signed) for them to simply hand us a check so we could leave this doesn’t seem like an attorney‘s office that is willing to make a smooth transaction and I would definitely never use them in the future. All other parties for this transaction were thoroughly confused why this attorney did not just sign paperwork with us especially since it is the same paperwork that they had to get signed with the buyers.

Jenn Blombeck and Beth Cook made our selling experience easy and frictionless. All went smoothly and Jenn kept my wife and I informed at all times during the process. A well-run, well-organized and personable law office.
I never spoke with the lawyer but Jenn, one of the paralegals, was great! She answered all my questions patiently and was very personable. I highly recommend.
I called for a consultation and this man was extremely rude. He spoke over me, very arrogant, and just outright rude. Don’t waste your time.

Lives up to his low review. Dismissive and condescending. Cannot be asked questions that challenge his infinite knowledge and wisdom. Rudely hung up on me. Remember, Mr Franco, everyone is a customer.
Called to consult with Mr. Franco on an ongoing matter and he was dismissive, unwilling to listen to me, spoke over and down to me continuously.
John Franco is a no nonsense attorney and has lots of experience. He gives solid legal counsel. I would highly recommend John Franco.

I've had the pleasure of collaborating with Angela over the past month as we develop our new commercial space. She has been instrumental in helping us review our commercial lease and in crafting sub-leases for our new premises.

As a first-time space holder, I don't claim to be an expert in this field. Angela's support has been a true blessing, simplifying this process for me. She has the ability to explain complex legal jargon in terms that I can easily grasp, and she's committed to ensuring that our relationship with the property owner is a true partnership, not a one-sided affair.

Whether you're a landlord or a commercial space holder, I wholeheartedly recommend reaching out to Angela to ensure you have all of your bases covered before you even realize you need them.

Best ever

reviews.text: I hardly ever write reviews, but my experience with Angela Z. has been (and continues to be) outstanding, so I can't keep her all to myself.  Her attention to my specific needs as a startup business in VT and needling through my lease was just the beginning.  I had trouble with my contractor and electrician during fit-up, and Angela guided me through the process of getting through what could've been rather ugly,  with the least cost (emotional and financial).  Without her and her consistently sound legal advice, my startup experience would have been excruciating.  THANK YOU ANGELA!

I've personally known and worked with Angela at Bennett & Zaikowski PC for about 3 years now and I am a very proud member of their Vermont Apartment Owners Association. Angela saved me from making a very costly mistake as I was previously pulling my leases for my rental units off of the internet! (DO NOT MAKE THAT SAME MISTAKE!) I quickly realized after speaking with Angela that I was at serious risk and now I have peace of mind in knowing that all of my leases are drafted by her firm and are meeting the Vermont law 100% . Angela has been such an amazing resource for random Tenant/Landlord questions that have come up over the years and she always takes time out of her busy day to promptly respond to my inquires. Thank you Angela, we truly appreciate all that you do for us!

reviews.text:
I highly recommend Tim and have used his services twice now-once for a closing on a single family home and once for a quitclaim deed. He is always very quick to respond, asks necessary questions to ensure he is providing us with appropriate counseling, and his rates are so reasonable. Personally, I found all our interactions to be professional, respectful, honest, and kind. I had called several local firms for closing quotes, and got slimy vibes from a few. So his straightforward demeanor was a breath of fresh air. He came highly recommended from our buyer's agent, and I'm so glad he was recommended to us! I will be calling Tim for any future legal needs.

It's worth noting that there were some hiccups on the seller's end at closing, and Tim set up a plan that protected our interests as buyers, while ensuring the sellers got funding in a timely manner. Tim helped minimize disruptions to the schedule and the funding in a way that worked well for both sides. We are grateful for his quick thinking and thankful to have had him representing us. I genuinely feel that our closing would not have happened if we had gone with a less experienced lawyer.

He was very considerate of my injury and took the extra time to meet with us, explain the process, and was available as needed to talk through our options.  We would highly recommend David for any personal injury legal assistance!

He was never offended by any of my questions, was very patient  and a good listener. He worked very hard and was very thorough with my case and I would recommend him to anyone who is dealing with a personal injury.

David Polow represented me following a motor vehicle collision in Morrisville, VT in which the other party was at fault. When I initially contacted Polow & Polow I was given a free consultation where I could explain what had happened and where David explained what he could do to help. The legal process was lengthy due, in part, to injuries I sustained in the crash and David would check in on me multiple times a month to see how my health was progressing. I really felt taken care of.  David's professionalism and experience in navigating the difficult waters both through the other driver's insurance company and my own was extremely reassuring and allowed me to just concentrate on healing. His receptionist, Heather, was always so cordial each and every time I called or stopped by their office. In the end, David's expertise led to a very handsome settlement and I could not be more grateful.

David Polow represented me in a law suite against a company responsible for causing a wreck in which I was involved. I received a broken wrist and a broken arm.  My car was also totaled. David got a good settlement for me. He was always professional and prompt in his handling of my case. I highly recommend him and his firm.

Davis Polow helped me tremendously in 2015-2016 when I had to sue my own insurance company due to the fact that I got hit by an uninsured motorist and my insurance company failed to cover my health and didn’t total my vehicle.  I was young only 19-20 years old and I had know idea how to go about the legal stuff. He made me comfortable and was extremely professional.

The accident left me with a permanent back complications till this day.

He helped me to go against this big cooperation and I was able to get something to help with my permanent back issues, without having to go to court.

I recommend this attorney because he is willing to fight for what is right and will stick with you until the end.

I will certainly use this attorney in the future, and I recommend him to anyone who has gotten hurt in a collision.

First the bad reviews here are nuts. They are irrelevant and Google should remove them. Personally, I think the hourly for ALL lawyers is insane, but relatively these folks are reasonable and don't waste time. I don't like that I always have to leave messages, but staffing is tough for all businesses and more staff means clients will pay more. I have worked with all of the lawyers in this firm and Nigel. I was looking for a lawyer so the VT Bar assigned me Ward law. At first, Ward law did not return calls. After several calls, they finally returned my call. Per the VT bar they were to give me 30 minutes of legal advice for $25 for the initial client contact. Nigel spent the entire 30 minutes describing why I need to pay every time a Lawyer breaths. Not 1 piece of legal advice was given. The simple question of do I have a case was not answered. They claimed they needed to research the law, and bill me, to determine if I have a case. I was horrified by the greed from this law firm and am looking for a way to report them to the VT bar for not giving me the 30 minutes of legal time they are required to to be a part of the VT bar association. They will freely take your money and provide you 0 legal advice.
5 stars are not nearly enough to acknowledge Chip's knowledge, guidance and patience.  Thank you, Chip.  Regards, Jeff

reviews.text: I've been seeking the assistance of attorney Will Towle and the other fine folks at Ward Law, PC for the better part of 12 years. If I could give no stars I would. I went above and beyond for chip on an application giving him documents for tenancy to be turned away (for no real reason). I asked for an honest answer on what made us not rentable.... we received nothing back. We had been rebuilding our credit had several thousand dollars in the bank (proved with statements) just to be shot down. He discriminated against us and stereotyped us for being young. Sorry we don’t drive around an Audi however we work very hard and pay our rent on time. Unless you want someone who is going to look down upon you DO NOT rent from him.
reviews.ownerResponse:
Jessica, you have never done business with our law firm. Your review is about applying to rent a house. We do not rent houses. We are a law firm.

Chip, one of our lawyers, personally manages a house which you apparently applied to be a tenant. This is unrelated to our law firm. It sounds like you were disappointed that you did not get the house, but it has nothing to do with our law firm or the services we provide.

An owner decides on an application based on information owners can gather about applicants. Poor credit, inadequate and unverifiable self employment income, being a smoker, a history of suing a past landlord, and generally not being forthright about rental history — these are all reasons a prospective tenant is not likely to be accepted by an owner.

reviews.text: This attorney office, when dealing with my funeral home, has always been pleasant and wonderful and prompt. I am unsure of their other skill sets as I have not experienced but if probate/estate things are needed, this is where I would suggest going.

We cannot recommend this organization, we hired a member attorney and the experience was very negative, from almost start to finish when we released them. We called a few other member attorneys. The knowledge base was very limited, and concerningly wrong in conversations with potential attorneys on more than one occasion, and on one occasion with a paralegal. It was disarming and alarming. Vermont is very small, not very competitive and options limited. I would recommend hiring from a larger firm out of state that has staff attorneys who are admitted in VT.
Hopefully this changes, for Vermonters and those seeking legal services in VT.  There is a miles long difference between capabilities and abilities and desire to be capable and able, from ethics to knowledge base, between attorneys in VT and more populated states. There is also an egotistical snobbery, in part because the state is so small, that does not get checked. It's almost if they come to VT to partly retire and not be true advocates or do any real work, and advance and evolve true justice, whatever that may be. They are also very unethically greedy, which is everywhere, but there is no foil to this in VT with more ethical practitioners and or firms, it is only greed based law, from the sole practitioner to the larger firms. Again due to the low population, there are less options and the ones there are the predominant ones in the field, greedy, not driven by high morality and standard / competence for themselves and clients, passionate - cause driven who knows, values, seeks the meaning in their work. A good attorney who is a true advocate is in the minority of the field.

I’ve known attorney Mark Kolter for many years — I met him through my parents before my own law school career even began. From the very start, he has always been the definition of “salt of the earth.” Approachable, kind, and genuinely the type of person you feel comfortable calling about anything.

Mark is incredibly honest, knowledgeable, and thorough. He takes the time to truly understand the issue and gives clear, thoughtful guidance. And just as importantly, if something is outside his scope, he doesn’t pretend otherwise — he knows exactly who can help and will point you in the right direction without hesitation.

I am an attorney myself, and I confidently refer everyone I know to Mark. That says everything. You’re not just getting a great lawyer — you’re getting a genuinely good human who cares about doing things the right way.
HAVE WORKED WITH MARK KOLTER FOR A LONG TIME AND HE HAS BEEN GREAT TO ME.....
Mark was a tremendous asset for me. He gave me sound advice so I could make a decision.

I had a tough personal injury case (slip and fall.) I contacted Mark Kolter after my claim was denied. He was honest with me from the start about the difficulties of proving these types of cases. But after hearing me out, presenting my side of the facts, he agreed to take my case.
I had 2 years of multiple surgeries on my neck and shoulder, PT, steroid injections, epidurals and on and on. It was the most painful 2 1/2 years of my life.
Mark listened to me, and most importantly, believed in me. He was attentive, thorough and honest. It was a complicated and difficult case. But he guided us through it with compassion, kindness and respect.
He was diligent, and with the help of the staff at the whole office, was in constant contact with us with updates and information to keep us informed on the status of our case.
With his many years of experience and working dynamic relationships with professional contacts, he was able to create a relationship with opposing counsel in a respectful manner that only helped our case.
After 2 1/2 years (from the date of my injury), we went to mediation and came away with a more than satisfactory settlement agreement. We could have chosen to continue to court, but with a 50/50 chance, and another year or two of court proceedings, we decided after thoughtful consideration, input from Mark and the experienced mediator, that the settlement was the best option.
My wife and I appreciate all the hard work Mark and the staff at the office put in. They were all kind, compassionate and diligent in their efforts. I wholeheartedly recommend speaking to Mark Kolter and this law office to handle your potential case.

I highly recommend Mark and the entire team at DKR for their exceptional handling of my husband’s personal injury case. From the beginning, they were incredibly supportive and kept us well-informed through every step of a this long process. What truly stood out for me, was Mark’s guidance during the mediation process. It wasn’t an easy time for us, but Mark’s expertise and supportive presence made all the difference. He provided clear, honest advice when we needed it most, and we felt truly advocated for throughout. Thank you for helping us through such a challenging time.

Doesn't hurt they are near a restaurant I now really enjoy!

I had an extremely disappointing and disturbing experience working with Mark Kolter. He was very dismissive, a bad communicator, and did not thoroughly review medical documentation in order to prepare me for my IME. He recommended things that were useless and expensive multiple times, instead of recommending helpful actions. I could not rely on him at all to effectively navigate this process. He couldn’t keep his cases straight and was always forgetting details and getting things mixed up. When I tried to address these issues in a matter-of-fact way, he attacked my character by calling me a rude angry person and dumped me as a client instead of admitting to and trying to correct his mistakes. I recommend avoiding this firm, and especially Mark, at all costs.

It's a pleasure to recommend E. Darby Herrington as a thorough, empathic, and excellent lawyer.  My recent experience with her began when I asked her to draft a pre-nuptial agreement between my husband-to-be, a Quebecer, and me, a US citizen.  My husband's and my goal was to make it clear to all that my assets were to remain mine, in the event of death or divorce after our marriage, so that no one--especially my three sons--would suspect that this marriage was about my assets.  Darby drafted a comprehensive and excellent agreement, which my husband was completely willing to sign.  We then learned that pre-nups don't hold much weight given Quebec's division of marital assets laws.  So, on Darby's advice, we turned in another direction, and drafted an irrevocable trust, placing the assets I identified into the trust, now held by my sons.  This work required several meetings, my sons were present for some of them, and throughout, Darby listened carefully and produced the documents we needed to accomplish our goals.  I will, without hesitation, use Darby's services in the future.  One additional note:  When I had a question about immigration law and in what country my husband and I should marry, Darby--not being well-versed in immigration law--gave me a referral that turned out to be excellent.  I mention this because it's evidence of Darby's integrity, I think, that she doesn't pretend to know about things that she doesn't know about.  I believe she's your best bet!  --Otho Thompson

Elizabeth with Anderson Lamb & Associates was a godsend for navigating the SSDI process. Applying for SSDI can be daunting. I filed my initial application myself and was initially denied. I reached out to Anderson Lamb & Associates for representation for my appeal and have zero regrets. Highly recommend...they are so helpful,  kind, patient and hard working.  They kept me informed and explained things every step of the way. I would say anyone trying to get their disability needs to have these lawyers on your case and by your side...don't go it alone.

My multi-year hellacious odyssey down the rabbit-hole of claiming disability benefits simply would not have been possible without the team at Anderson Lamb & Associates.  Starting with my initial phone call, I was given much needed reassurance, and the process which would unfold and all of its possible permutations was explained to me in detail in very plain language.  Once the process got underway, my representative Elizabeth A. Thabault was a tireless advocate: always available for questions and concerns, always prompt and encouraging in all of her responses, always communicative in regards to every development in the case.  They tirelessly pursued all necessary documentation, medical and otherwise, while we prepared for my hearing.   After eloquently and tenaciously making my case in front of an extremely prejudiced judge and deftly countering every point made by the “expert” witness, Elizabeth and my team were called upon to pursue the case into a higher court after an unfounded unfavorable decision.  They were able to keep my involvement in that stressful process to an absolute minimum, with very precise and simple input as necessary from me.  Given the amount of trauma I was enduring while trying to adjust to becoming disabled, the fact that my case was in capable hands allowed me peace of mind for which I could not otherwise have hoped.

The amount of effort and care exhibited by everyone at Anderson Lamb & Associates, especially Elizabeth Thabault, cannot be overstated, nor can the compassion, understanding and encouragement that was available at every stage of the journey.  Masterful legal representation, a sympathetic ear and a passionate pursuit of  justice are each rare enough on their own, but Elizabeth and the team at Anderson Lamb showed an abundance of all of them, making this ordeal into something I could successfully weather.
Such a great law firm they fought so hard for me and ill never forget what u guys have done for me

reviews.text: I hired Rob Backus to battle an unlawful traffic violation. He knew it was bogus from the start and I suspected so as well. The judge quickly threw the case out once we had a hearing because of his extensive knowledge and expertise in this matter. He made a very good case. He was very kind, helpful, and confident and quickly got back to me when I had questions or concerns. I had plenty of questions and concerns and his replies were right to the point with comforting overtones which really helped my state of mind in these challenging times. Life is hard enough without having to worry about police injustice so it's good to know someone will go to bat for you when injustice strikes. He was also reasonably priced which just puts the icing on the cake. I would certainly hire him again if I ever had to!
reviews.ownerResponse: Thank you for your feedback. We appreciate hearing from you Todd, glad Rob could help you with your case!

I came to Nicole with an old trust someone else had put together, that I thought I just needed an amendment for. Although I already had a trust there was still so much I didn't understand about the legal language.  Nicole answered all my questions, even when I asked them over again for clarification.
Properly putting together a trust is not an easy thing and there are so many details that only a trust lawyer would know about including.  I feel she did an excellent job restating my trust and I feel I now have a good understanding of it.  And if I still have a question - I just need to ask her.

reviews.ownerResponse: The individual providing this review is not a client of Krystn Perettine nor McCormick, Fitzpatrick, Kasper & Burchard, P.C. Rather, the named reviewer is an adverse party in a pending legal matter and seemingly not in agreement with my client's role and position in the case. Due to the lack of a client relationship and the adverse nature of the reviewer's relationship with our firm's client, we have asked Google to remove this review and will be providing Google with documentation to support that request.

reviews.text:
Stay away from this firm. They are attempting to disassociate themselves from any client relationship with us to remove negative reviews. However, they have indeed been actively involved with our family estate since Augustof 2024. We have had significant interactions, including meetings, calls, and extensive correspondence. It appears they are resorting to desperate measures as other stakeholders within the company may not be pleased with attorneys actions.
It is unfortunate that we are faced with this issue, but it is essential to address it head-on when dealing with such challenges. In my opinion and based on my experience, this conduct displays a lack of integrity, questionable ethics, and a failure to uphold fiduciary duty. I have observed a lackadaisical approach for almost a year, and recent attempts to remove negative but honest feedback from the public domains are concerning.
The persistent lack of communication and other behaviors have prompted further investigation, including legal consultations and conversations with relevant authorities. As per Vermont law and guidance obtained from the Vermont Court, her actions are deemed unacceptable and will be addressed accordingly.
reviews.ownerResponse: The individual providing this review is not a client of Krystn Perettine nor McCormick, Fitzpatrick, Kasper & Burchard, P.C. The first lawyer we had was in over his head and looked at me like my honor roll student is going to jail for a long time, even after showing him the evidence we had.   We fired him and hired Mr.Langrock, and were very grateful he took our case.  He told me, trust me, It’s going to take time but believe I know your son is innocent and we will get through this.  It was hard dealing with an incompetent states attorney (DW), but Mr.Langrock kept his composure.  In the end my son was unscathed, and Mr.Langrock was true to his word.  I am forever indebted to him for all he did for my family.   It’s very scary when you know your son is innocent and the courts were so back logged that to the judge and States Attorney it was like moving cows through a parlor, they treated everyone as guilty.   Very eye opening and I can only wonder and weep at  how many innocent sons, daughters, fathers etc that  couldn’t afford a good lawyer are now sitting in jail or convicted of bogus charges.   If you are innocent, Langrock is the man you want.  He will fight for you because he knows how many people get wrongly convicted and feels it’s his duty as a lawyer to make sure the truth is known.  Thank you Mr.Langrock, I can sleep good at night again!  I’ll never forget the first time walking into court with you after we fired the first lawyer.  All of the sudden there was respect and 2 state’s attorney’s because they knew they were in for it when Langrock walked in.  Very quick witted and not to be pushed around.  We can’t thank you enough!   Hope all is well buddy!   Thanks again!
The best lawyer in Vermont.

Peter Langrock was to have represented me in two cases, a civil case where I was being sued frivolously, and a criminal case of “he said she said” brought on by the same parties. He delayed the criminal case against my wishes so that he could bring a motion on the civil before Judge Allen Chever, whom he leaked to me was his client. The civil case was a clear cut dismissal, not a lucrative trial for a lawyer. The plaintiffs were trying to unilaterally modify my deed -- it would invalidate deeds. After leaking that Cheever was Langrock's client, Peter Langrock had me waive my right to a jury in the criminal case involving the same plaintiffs as the civil which put both cases in the hands of his client, the judge. But, at criminal trial, Langrock called me down off the witness stand WITHOUT CROSS EXAMINING ME, his own client in a he said she said. This is so negligent that I must be public about my case. In his closing defense, Langrock then added that he wished we were in Scotland where the laws were different. I was falsely convicted. Then, Allen Chever ruled against my deed, and instead of granting summary judgment in my favor, he denied the motion which was to force a costly trial for me and a lucrative one for his lawyer, Peter Langrock. I fired Peter and Fritz, his son now on the ABA Board of Governors in Vermont. I was able to represent myself on the civil suit and win without trial because I had a deed that could not be altered, and a new judge took the case. I was able to do better without attorney than having been represented by Peter Langrock and Fritz Langrock.
Not willing to help, 5000 retainer fee, friendly and listened, did offer suggestions .  But a bit pricey unless it's serious..

I am sure if you are lucky enough to be chosen by Ms. Ready she is a good lawyer, but she is too busy to provide adequate service to new clients. I was given advice to get a continuance which I worked hard to get so I could get a counseling meeting a month in advance, then I was told by Katina that oops I am sorry but I am too busy and can't represent you. Very disappointing.

Katina was my Public Defender and she had my best interest in mind and truly care about my outcome , long after her services were rendered I still to this day see her and she always asks how I'm doing . Katina is the only person i trusted, feeling she understood the position i was in , not judged

John Brady of Brady & Callahan represented me as a defendant in a pedestrian / motor vehicle accident, an accident where someone else in another car hit the pedestrian; a  spurious claim if there ever was one.

Beginning with depositions and continuing at trial Plaintiffs' counsel was very aggressive, often obnoxiously so. Mr. Brady was very adept at defusing his preposterous claims to the jury.

I will never forget his summation to the Jury: "Ladies & Gentlemen  in considering who is responsible consider this; if Mr. Plaintiff had hesitated for one second, just looked both ways…we wouldn't be here today".  The "not responsible" verdict in our favor took less than an hour. Mr. Brady's ability to succinctly identify the pivotal issue in the case and persuasively present it  to the jury won the day.

Over the last 40 years in business I have dealt with many lawyers. Few if any are as intelligent and articulate as Mr. Brady.

I recommend him without reservation.

The experience I had with Brady & Callahan Law was nothing but professional and very friendly, worked hard for my case, made things clear for me to understand. Brendan Donahue is very professional, very effitiant, worked very hard to help me get what i deserved!! Would recommend anyone to use Brady & Callahan!

My wife had an unfortunate accident that left her disabled and that was work related but the workmen's comp insurance company refused to pay for it.  It took us over a year and two different legal firms before we hooked up with Brendan at Brady/Callahan.  Brendan is as nice a young man as you will ever meet and an excellent attorney.  He was right there with us every step of the way.  It took three and a half years but the workmen's comp insurance company finally accepted the facts and paid.  We were lucky to find him and will always be grateful for the work they did on our behalf.  You can't find a better firm to put your trust in.

Update 2026 - my case was rather tricky but every door that open Kenny fought by my side. She knows all the right words to say. I did settle my case in 2022 before moving & every second I needed her after she was still available. They keep records up to 6 years just case you need to come back.

I appreciate everything Michelle did for me. I would hire her over & over again.

reviews.text:
I am sharing my personal experience with Nancy Corsones so that anyone considering hiring her or working with her professionally understands the long-term consequences her decisions can have on families. My experience with her began in a custody case when my son was very young. If you’re questioning my credibility, I encourage you to read the 2016 Seven Days article titled “Court Finds Abuse Claims Baseless but a Mom Still Can’t See Her Son” by Ken Picard. It outlines part of what happened.

Over the years, I complied with every legal order, told the truth, and presented clear evidence — including findings from multiple law enforcement agencies and medical professionals. Despite all of this, Ms. Corsones made decisions that kept me separated from my son, even though the accusations against me were thoroughly investigated and found to be false by agencies including Homeland Security, a Special Investigations Unit, and the FBI.

One of the most distressing aspects of my experience was her refusal to acknowledge critical mistakes — even when presented with medical proof. At one point, she disregarded the medical opinion of my board-certified OB-GYN and accused me, without basis, of causing harm during pregnancy. When I obtained genetic testing to disprove the accusation, rather than correcting the record, she became adversarial. Even after her rulings were reversed by a higher court, she continued to act as if they were valid.

Ultimately, the impact of her choices has been permanent: my adult son is estranged from me and believes harmful falsehoods, despite the fact that I was never charged or convicted of any crime. The damage done by prolonged separation and misinformation is irreparable.

Now that my son is grown and outside of the legal system, I’m finally free to speak out. I hope this review helps others make informed decisions when choosing legal professionals. My experience with Ms. Corsones was devastating, and I would not wish it on anyone.

Wendy was the best choice I could have made for myself to be my representation during my legal journey.

I had never gone through a divorce before, let-alone any legal process. Wendy is not only incredibly intelligent, but she's concise, no-nonsense, to the point, and efficient.

She does not waste time, was and is always in communication with me to ensure I am privy to the process every step along the way. Wendy has not only been my representation, but she's been my backbone throughout, and is able to show me the forest through the trees in what is already an emotional process for myself.

I was able to put full trust and confidence in her collaborative decision making with myself, while she was aware that I was coping with the emotional side of it all.

The divorce process is never easy (not that I can imagine, anyway). However, knowing that Wendy was going through this process with me made me feel supported, empowered and strong. She was always in my corner, prompt with decision making and information gathering, and always in communication with me.

I could never have done this without her. Not only did I end this journey with a positive result (and a quick one at that), but I now feel stronger for it thanks to Wendy's presence and guidance.

Both Nancy and Wendy are extremely knowledgeable, forthcoming and financially reasonable in handling your legal needs.  Not only do they offer the necessary professional acumen;  they are both extremely caring, empathetic and supportive.  These traits in legal counsel are necessary when navigating the long and painful process of divorce.   Thank you both!

Wendy is an incredible asset to have on your side. Her transparency, expertise, and extensive knowledge of Vermont family law  made all the difference to my court experience. I would recommend Corsones and Fitzsimons to all.

Wendy is very honest and straightforward. She was easy to work with and would explain things thoroughly. I would recommend this service to anyone seeking legal help.

Her demeanor is warm and caring and she takes a genuine interest in the human side of her cases. I consider myself very fortunate to have found Wendy and that she was able and willing to take my case.

Nancy Corsones, her partner at the law firm, gave me sound advice and encouragement.
I am deeply indebted to them.
Wendy is knowledgeable, professional and responsive.

We recently worked with Don, and I cannot speak highly enough about the experience. He helped my family and me navigate a very complex situation, and his guidance throughout the entire process was invaluable.

Don is extremely knowledgeable and was consistently available whenever we needed him. His communication was clear, timely, and thorough — he always made sure we understood every step, every option, and what each potential path meant for us. What I appreciated most was his transparency and the way he laid out our choices without ever pushing us in any particular direction. My wife was severely injured in a traffic crash when a distracted driver disregarded a traffic control device, leaving her with permanent physical disabilities. I hired Ted Kramer and his firm to represent me in an extraordinarily complex foreclosure lawsuit involving SBA loans secured by my Vermont home. A bank filed the action, and Ted guided me through a multi-year legal battle spanning multiple states and law firms.

I won the lawsuit in Chicago, eliminating over $1,000,000 in SBA loan liability and $1,200,000 in the bank’s legal costs.

This was not a simple case. Over more than four years, Ted coordinated with four attorneys from my Chicago legal team, my Vermont real estate attorney, and opposing counsel, all while personally managing every aspect of the Vermont litigation.

Because of Ted’s work with the legal team, the lien on my home was fully removed, allowing me to complete the sale of the property. He stayed involved from start to finish—through dismissal of the lawsuit and all the way to the final closing.

Ted is sharp, relentless, professional, and completely trustworthy. I would hire him again without hesitation for any high-stakes legal matter and recommend him to anyone facing serious, complex litigation.

reviews.text: I know Kurt Mausert both as an attorney and as a person. Kurt has helped me at times and did very well by me. Unfortunately we had a case that tore us apart that created some animosity. That being said we both could have done somethings different and have now come to agree to disagree like gentlemen. I have recently followed a very high profile and unpopular case which Kurt is representing the defendant. I have to say that the way Kurt is professionally, zealously and blindly representing his client is not only impressive but admirable especially in small town USA. Now I have learned that he, his family and his practice is being attacked because of this case which is completely unfair and inappropriate. The Constitution affords us all the right to a defense and be presumed innocent until proven guilty in a court of law by a jury of our peers in any matter no matter the severity amongst several other rights that are inalienable. We can not pick and chose which rights can and  cannot be enjoyed by whom and when those rights can and cannot be enjoyed under a circumstance of our choosing. If the later was the case these would not be rights they would be privileges' and most likely enjoyed only by the elite up until they fell out of favor with their peers. God forbid any of us found ourselves in a position whether purposefully or accidently to have to exercise our rights to a defense we would all want our counsel to advocate for us as zealously as Kurt is for his client.  Please stop harassing him, his family and defaming his business just for simply doing his job and doing it the best he can. As a business owner I would not want this to happen to me as I am sure you wouldn't want this to happen to you or someone you love. Lastly as a business owner I would want my employees to work as hard as the could to be the best at what they are and or can be, because this is how we become successful as individuals, organizations and as a society as a whole.

He’s my father, but he has also represented me on several speeding tickets (do not underestimate a Volvo 240DL). Call me biased, but he has more integrity than any other attorney anywhere, ever. His commitment to defending every client ethically and to the best of his ability is total, and he has fought (and won) against a corrupt and well-connected District Attorney when nobody else would. He also routinely represents kids in family court and cares deeply about that work. He fights on principle and with an expert understanding of the law to keep the legal system honest in every case, and zealously advocates for every client with tenacity and compassion.
I have known Kurt Mausert for several years. I appreciate his vast knowledge, compassion, wisdom and integrity. He has given us excellent advice over the years. Thank you, Kurt!

He also told me a couple days ahead that if the case did not go to court he would only take 26% of what I received. Right after the deposition which was carried out in his office, he tried to take 33%. I reminded him what he told me. Then he took 26%. I have a history of depression which is why I was there anyway. My employer was a hospital. They had a big shot attorney who attempted to make me look like a criminal. The law system is so messed up. I believe attornies work together with kickbacks involved if the settlement is much less than what it should be. My case was truly a case of discrimination. Too many details to go into right here. I am very disappointed in my situation. It took me quite a while to write this review. I had reservations about it but I feel like people need to know especially those in a similar situation. reviews.text:
James Maswick, of Flink Maswick Law, is the reason for bad lawyer jokes. There are no words sufficient enough to express what a terrible job he did for our property closing. Luckily, not a penny of our money went into his pocket. My husband and I were the property sellers, and James Maswick was the attorney for the buyers. What should have been a two week timeframe to closing turned into over three months due to James's negligent lack of professionalism. It was a cash deal on a small vacant parcel on a pond in northern NY. It started with the buyers attorney asking for a 30 day extension of due diligence in order to look up specs on code for building a residence on the property (which were already in the deed). At the end of that 30 day period, James still had not looked into any specs for his clients and asked for another 10 days. Throughout both of these processes, James obviously was not doing his job and would go "missing" for days or even a week at a time in order to drag it out longer. This 30 day due diligence period went on for over three months, literally, through all of his unprofessionalism and lack of communication. He kept coming up with "issues" that his office already had answers and paperwork for, but had gone unread. Even a month after a very long and drawn out closing, we realized that James had not actually contacted the people his clients supposedly had asked for paperwork from. He lied about certain circumstances in order to buy himself more time, which he never used to address his clients concerns.
We wouldn't wish business dealings with James Maswick on our worst enemies (okay, well maybe a few enemies!). His performance was beyond unprofessional. He was inconsiderate, uncaring, pompous, and honestly, both my husband and I have no idea how he retains licensing in the state of NY.
For the record, I have bought and sold real estate, without an attorney or an agent, in three separate states before this transaction, and working with James was, by FAR, the worst experience in real estate I have ever had.
Jamey is fantastic! A great attorney and handles real estate transactions well.
reviews.ownerResponse: Thank you John!  It's a pleasure doing business with you!

I used Stackpole & French for the Probate for my brother's Estate. They were professional and very responsive in each of our interactions. Their counsel was welcomed as some of the situations or Vermont  laws were unknown to me as an out-of-state person filing for a Vermont resident. Our experience with Ed French and the entire team at Stackpole & French Law Offices was excellent.

We came to them with an emotionally sensitive buyout involving family members.

From the first call, Ed was steady, clear, empathetic, and calm; he made a difficult chapter manageable, and we’re deeply grateful for his, and his team’s support.

We recommend Stackpole & French without hesitation.

My son and I recently sold our property located in Cambridge, Vermont, after having owned it as a second home (we are Canadians) for forty-five years. To assist us in this undertaking, we retained a team of professionals at the firm of Stackpole & French, composed of Clay Thomas, Kristal Griswold and Laurie Waterhouse . Throughout the process of preparing the necessary documentation, they maintained  direct contact with us and with the purchasers' attorneys, in a very effective and expeditious way so that on the date of closing, the necessary papers were signed and executed quickly and funds exchanged correctly, to our satisfaction.We are pleased to recommend  this team of professionals for this type of transaction.
Wasted my time. Zero Help. Thanks for nothing!
Struggled to understand our needs, and to support successful solutions for our situation. Costly.

Pleasant, prompt and efficient. I am from out of state and needed help moving a property from my recently passed father. Marlene stayed right on top of things, frequently checking in over the process, which was extended due to my own situation, with estates and elder care, and made sure things got done.

We used Stackpole & French as attorneys for a property sale. Sale of the home closed in September and funds transfered to the law office. After multiple emails and speaking with no less than 4 assistants (Clay, Kristal, Marlene, Laurie) I was finally able to get a bank transfer of the proceeds of the sale in November.

After confirming my wire details over the phone for the third time I was told the original person who had been helping me had been on vacation (for 3 weeks?).

Simple communication like confirming addresses and bank account numbers took multiple emails, calls and conversations with different people who were not up to date or synchronized with the process.

The inefficiency of this office made for a slow, painful and frustrating experience.

Marlene & Sarah were excellent and very wonderful to work with.
I greatly appreciate all their help with my father's estate.  I strongly recommend Stackpole and French.

Just not very good.  Contract took far too long to be drawn up and cost 3x what most other lawyers would have charged.  I like Ed, but some of the attorneys under him were very inefficient.  Also, the office has a fairly high rate of turnover, never a good sign.   Unfortunately, not recommended.
Stackpole and French are great!  Will our attorney was very professional and knowledgeable!  They were very responsive  with communication and reasonable for the work provided.  Highly recommended!

We have had a very pleasant experience with Stackpole & French. We needed to update our trust docs prior to traveling in January.  Erin and her team were responsive, and punctual. We sucessfully updated our docs in a speedy and friendly fashion.

I've used Stackpole & French for over a dozen business transactions and there is good reason why I continue to work with them. Their professionalism, personal care and communication are top notch. You get what you pay for in this field, and Stackpole & French have always exceeded this in my eyes.

I was very pleased with my experence withStackpole and French.  I had no idea how to start with my extate planning and how involved it could be.  They took the time to walk me through everything, explaining as we went along.  It was a very positive experence and everyone was very supportive.

We love the whole team at Stackpole & French. No matter our need, they offer the top level degree of professionalism, strategy and efficiency through the whole process. Plus, everyone is just so warm and delightful. :) Highly recommend.

did a great job helping me with my traffic ticket! thanks Kaplan and Kaplan!!!

When a criminal defense attorney was needed most, Robert Kaplan and the entire KaplanLaw team were available and delivered 5-Star service in all categories.

very unfair business practices. Withheld client information during dispute.

Mike Munson, Al Dean and all others were professional, caring, and extremely determined to resolve my situation. I would use them time and time again. They were amazing with communication and never had to question any decision they would suggest. If you need a lawyer, go to them.

I highly recommend Barr Sternberg and Atty Alexander Dean. He was forthright, knowledgeable, and actually came up with a winning strategy to help me settle my legal matters in a fair manner.

I'm Court appointed this Attorney Christopher Montgomery from this law firm and all this lawyer largely does is make insults at me about every time I interacted with him save for initial contact, doesn't seem to have checked previous attorney effectiveness and today sent an email asking me to remove this review or he'll withdraw and then in the same token asks for a document the previous attorney should have forwarded him which he hasnt forwarded to me though he said he would like a month ago, as I claimed he lied about records I gave him by claiming they were from Attn Kathleen Rivers, did not follow directive and offered a plea without my direction a week ago and admits the Court is just going to convict me, claimed I wasn't communicating and claimed I wanted him off case but said he wouldn't withdraw yet, and missed a hearing date where I had to waive and defend myself. I have nothing but incredible things to say about Chris and Maureen. They are the most professional, heard-working team I have ever come across. Their responsiveness is second to none and I'm very grateful to them both for managing my case.

My experience with Attorney Chris Montgomery from this LawFirm was Amazing! I’ve always had a distrust for Attorneys and thought they were all greedy and liars but Attorney Chris changed that belief. He represented a family member in a very complicated criminal matter that seemed hopeless without jail time however he got him NO jail time and probation!
He was very thorough and honest from the very beginning and never made a promise he couldn’t keep.

reviews.text: Excellent Attorney.  David Silver wraps his mind around things quickly.   Gets things done.  He takes the time to understand the situation and to communicate with clients.  Takes his job very seriously.  He knows the law.  Highly recommended.  He will take care of you.

Lisman lies and understands that the legal system is not an ethical system but one of asset exchange and assignment among preferred participants....one that can absorb an enormous amount of deceit. He's commoditized his Harvard credential and his political affiliations to make Vermont's legal system unbearable. It's little more than brutal careerists with the occasional subnormal and comedic relief (Skoglund). Congratulations on financial success at the expense of what makes life worth living.
Lisman work disrupted a homeowners community.  Greed.

This was the first time I needed a lawyer and didn't know who to see or what to expect. I was given Bill Leckerling's name from someone who highly recommended him, and I was not disappointed. He was very kind, professional, and got the results I needed. I also need to note his wonderful staff who were very curtious and helped me through the entire process. Thank you.

Unfortunately, I suffered a ski lift loading injury. After the dust settled, I received a recommendation from a family friend to reach out to Bill Leckerling, Esq. I met Bill, which was one of the best decisions I have ever made. From the moment I sat down with him, I knew he genuinely cared about my wellbeing and wanted to help me recoup my financial losses. He was extremely knowledgeable and diligent in his review of the case material, investigation, seeking out appropriate experts and preparation for trial. Pam Pinard, paralegal, is an integral part of the team who works diligently and tirelessly to make sure everything stays on track. They are both always available and extremely quick to respond to any questions or concerns.  Bill’s outlook on the case was spot on from the very beginning and his help and guidance was extremely helpful. His depositions were nothing short of brilliant and clearly guaranteed me a successful outcome. I therefore highly recommend Bill Leckerling and his team for any personal injury case.

After speaking with multiple lawyers in the Burlington area, I had this feeling that I was just a number to them and that didn't sit right with me.  With a family/friend recommendation I met Bill and Pam at Lisman Leckerling.  The moment I sat down with them I felt nothing but a pure genuine interest of helping me though this difficult time. The extensive knowledge and insight around my particular case that Bill had to offer was above and beyond my expectations. Bill gave me options and guidance, but above all Bill and Pam gave me peace of mind. Bill set the expectations from the very beginning which didn't leave any unanswered questions. Pam was an integral part of this process with staying on top of every fine detail. I honestly could go on more, but what I'm trying to convey here is this, If you are looking for a professional and an extremely genuine lawyer with unparalleled integrity give Bill a call and see and hear for yourself!
Mr. Leckerling assisted me with a products liability issue. He was knowledgeable, professional, and compassionate. Staff at Lisman Leckerling PC were efficient and courteous. I highly recommend them.

I had Attorney Kozlowski in the Lisman Leckerling office represent me on an extremely complex probate case in a different county that took years for the executor to finalize.  He is extremely competent, dedicated, fair and he explains complex financials clearly.  Among other corrections he made along the way, he realized the estate taxes had been filed incorrectly and corrected them.  If I could give him ten stars I certainly would.
The caring and compassion of Mr leckerling and his staff were amazing . The dedication to me was amazing. I would recommend him highly.

When my husband was injured I sought out advice from friends and family as to an attorney to represent us in a medical malpractice lawsuit. Bill Leckerling was the name that kept coming up. I met with Bill and he was very supportive. I approached him at a time when my husband was in the hospital and the prognosis didn't look good. He patted my hand while I cried in his office. Then, he asked me pertinent questions about my husband's health and what had happened to him just prior to the hospitalization. When he agreed to take the case I was very relieved because I knew that my husband Greg and I would get justice. Bill is like a bull dog. Once he grabs on, he doesn't let go. He researched every possibility about the issues that we had and came up with very viable solutions and then pursued every avenue. He didn't stop until we, his clients, were completely satisfied. We sued the responsible party and we had a positive outcome. All the while, for over 2 years, Bill was 110% with us. He did an outstanding job during the presentation phase of the case, proving that the other party was responsible for my husband's loss of fingers and toes. It's been 6 years, and while my husband will never be the man he was, at least we have been able to pick up our lives and move forward, and I give Bill Leckerling the credit for helping us do that. Bill is certainly the best medical malpractice lawyer not only in knowledge, but also in grit, style, substance, understanding the in's and out's of the case, and in making us feel comfortable and well informed as to what would happen next. You can lose your money here, or can put it towards a lawyer that answers questions, returns emails, updates you on what actions were taken on your part, and not make the case worse than it already was due to his utter lack of due diligence.
